person.name: Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde

nameForms.fullText: Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde

parts.value: Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde

burialPlace.address: Wymondham, South Norfolk District, Norfolk, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Served the Duke of Bedford when he fought in France as regent during Henry VI's minority (1413), and was his "second chamberlain", 1425 took part in a train against Anjou, 1433-34 commander of the fortress of Vire in Normandy, and became 1433 naturalized by Parliament (Henry VII), 1445 applied to the King of England to take possession of an inheritance of movable and immovable property accruing to him in Denmark, in England the name was Sir Andrew Ogarde (Aagaard), lived 1454, probably died 1455.
Unnamed letter writers, his relatives, proposed in 1458 to the widow of "the renowned Andreas Agardis throughout England and several kingdoms", according to his last will, a marriage between one of their daughters, when the daughter became an adult, and a son of Mr. Axel Pederssøn (Thott), in order to prevent her children's paternal inheritance from being split up, as the children's paternal uncles and also Mr. Peder Eriksen, whom the widow had appointed as their guardian, had died, and therefore requested her to negotiate with the transferor Mr. Peder Friis, canon in Ribe, and advised sending the daughter over here in time, so that she could learn the customs and language of the country.
In 1460, Mr. Anders Pedersen's children are mentioned in Erik Eriksen Løvenbalk's and his brother's probate either in Aagaard or in movable property with their maternal nephew Mourids Nielsen after their mother's mother, Miss. Karin. Cf. Will. Christensen, Missive II 7 ff.
Source: https://finnholbek.dk/getperson.php?personID=I44106&tree=2

About Mr. Anders' Descendants Haggard of Bradenham. Bokenham Castle, County Norfolk, bearing a six-pointed silver star, see Pedigree of David Haggard (1530) in B. Burke, History of the gentry, I 1898, 647 f.

The Gyldenstierne family, one of Denmark's oldest, most famous and most powerful nobles, has taken its name from its telling shield mark, a seven-pointed gold star in blue; the helmet sign shows two iron-clad arms holding a gold-rimmed blue mirror studded with 7 peacock feathers.
The family's mark points to a common origin with Hvide, Pors and other Jewish families.
Source: Denmark's Nobility Yearbook 1926 - (Finn Holbek ed.) (Col.), as cited in https://finnholbek.dk/showmedia.php?mediaID=542&medialinkID=35064

In the mid-15th century, Andrew Ogard (naturalized as an English citizen) built the Rye House, what most consider to be the first brick house built in Great Britain post-Roman Empire.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde於大約1409年出生在丹麥奧爾波斯凱特盧普時，他的父親Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard42歲，母親Gertsdatter Ulfstand33歲。他於大約1433年與Alice Lovell在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1454年10月13日在英國英格蘭享年46歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾福爾可維蒙達哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde于大约1409年出生在丹麦奧爾波斯凱特盧普时，他的父亲Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard42岁，母亲Gertsdatter Ulfstand33岁。他于大约1433年与Alice Lovell在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1454年10月13日在英国英格兰享年46岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾福爾可維蒙達哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Pedersen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Niels, Bjorn, Knud, Nils, Anders, Jorgen, Egil, Einer, Knut, Peer.
Norwegian and Danish: patronymic from the personal name Peder (see Peter ). This surname is also found in northern Germany. Compare Petersen 2 and Peterson 1.
English: variant of   Peterson  .

variants.name: Pederson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Hjalmer, Obert, Ove, Hilmer, Iver, Knud, Lars, Selmer, Alf, Anders.
Americanized form of Norwegian and Danish Pedersen or of the Swedish cognate Pedersson.
English: variant of   Peterson  .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

nameMeanings.name: a.k.a.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (from North Africa): from the male personal name ʿAdda, used in the Maghreb, a pet form of any of the many names beginning with ʿAbd ‘servant’.
Turkish: ornamental or topographic name from ada ‘island’.
Filipino: unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Sier  , unexplained. This surname is very rare in Germany.
Czech (Šír): nickname from Middle High German schīr ‘sheer, pure, clean’. Compare Schier .

variants.name: Sier
meanings: Dutch and German: from a short form of the personal name Sierik, Sierich, composed of the ancient Germanic elements sigi ‘victory’ + rīc ‘power(ful)’, or from a short form of the personal name Sigihard, composed of the ancient Germanic elements sigi + hari ‘army’.
English: variant of   Syer  .

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the usual vernacular English form (recorded from the 13th century onward) of the New Testament Greek personal name Andreas . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech Ondřej (see Ondrey ) and Albanian Ndreu , and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see examples at Andrews ).
History: The surname Andrew was first brought to North America from England by Robert Andrew (died 1668), who settled in Boxford, MA.

variants.name: Danson
meanings: English (Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Dand, a Middle English and Older Scots rhyming pet form of both Andrew and Randolph , with -son.

variants.name: Andrews
meanings: English: variant of Andrew , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This is the usual southern English patronymic form, also found in Wales; the Scottish and northern English form is Anderson . In North America, this surname has absorbed various cognates from other languages, e.g. Polish Andrzejewski , Slovenian Andrejčič, Serbian and Croatian Andrić (see Andric ), and Czech Ondráček (see Ondracek ).
Irish and Scottish: Anglicized form of Scottish Gaelic Mac Aindreis or Irish Mac Aindriú, see McAndrew .

History: This was a common name among the early settlers in New England. Robert Andrews emigrated in 1635 from Norwich, England, to Ipswich, MA. Even before 1635, one Thomas Andrews is recorded as being established in Hingham. A certain William Andrews was a member of John Davenport's company, which sailed from Boston in 1638 to found the New Haven colony.

variants.name: Andreas
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Heinz, Otto, Beate, Bernd, Ernst, Ilse, Kurt.

German and English: from the New Testament Greek name Andreas, which gave rise to Andrew in English and vernacular derivatives in almost every other European language. Etymologically, it is from Greek andreios ‘manly’, an adjective derivative of anēr (genitive andros) ‘man, male’. This is a Greek translation of an unknown Aramaic original; it was the name of the first of Christ's disciples, the patron saint of both Scotland and Russia. The Scottish surname Anderson is far more common than its Russian equivalent, Andreev. The personal name was popular throughout Europe in various vernacular forms (including Italian Andrea, French André, Spanish Andrés, Scottish, North German, and Scandinavian Anders, Dutch Andries, Hungarian András, Czech Ondřej, Polish Andrzej and Jędrzej, and Russian Andrey).
Americanized or Germanized form of Slovenian Andrejaš: from an old vernacular form of the personal name Andraž (from Latin Andreas), which is a variant of Andrej; see Andrew .

variants.name: Anderson
meanings: Scottish and northern English: patronymic from the personal name Ander(s), a northern Middle English form of Andrew , + son ‘son’. The frequency of the surname in Scotland is attributable, at least in part, to the fact that Saint Andrew is the patron saint of Scotland, so the personal name has long enjoyed great popularity there. Legend has it that the saint's relics were taken to Scotland in the 4th century by a certain Saint Regulus. In North America, this surname has absorbed many cognate or like-sounding surnames in other languages, notably Scandinavian (see 3 and 4 below), but also Ukrainian Andreychenko etc.
German: patronymic from the personal name   Anders  , hence a cognate of 1 above.
Americanized form (and a less common Swedish variant) of Swedish   Andersson  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Andy
meanings: English: from a pet form of the personal names   Andrew   or   Andreas  .
Native American (mainly Yakama): adoption of the English personal name Andy (see 1 above) as a surname.

nameMeanings.name: Ogarde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Vasant, Ajay, Anand, Tanuja.
Basque: habitational name from a place so named in Navarre, from Basque garde ‘place where wild oats grow’.
English and German: variant of   Gard  .

variants.name: Bellegarde
meanings: French (mainly southwestern): habitational name from (La) Bellegarde or (La) Belle Garde, names of several places in various parts of France, e.g. Bellegarde-du-Gard, named with Old French garde ‘watch, protection’ (see Garde ) and designating a stronghold. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Belgard and Belgarde .

variants.name: Gardea
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Raul, Jesus, Juanita, Leticia, Ramon, Ricardo, Sergio, Alejandro, Armando, Corina.
Basque: variant of   Garde  .

variants.name: Gardell
meanings: Americanized form of German Gardill, which is unexplained.
Swedish: ornamental name, at least in some cases from the Gotlandic placename element gard ‘enclosure’ + the suffix -ell, a derivative of the Latin adjectival ending -elius.
Altered form of French Gardel or Gardelle, both derivatives of Garde .

variants.name: Gard
meanings: English (southern): occupational name from Middle English gard(e), guard(e) ‘care, custody, protection’ (Old French garde ‘guard, watchman’), for a member of a town watch or troop of guards.
Norwegian (also Gård): habitational name from any of the two farmsteads so named in Rogaland, from Old Norse garthr ‘farm’.
Swedish (Gård): topographic or ornamental name from gård ‘farm’.

variants.name: Gardon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Rodolfo, Julio, Vicente.
French: from a derivative of Old French garde ‘watch, protection’ (see Garde ).

Spanish (Gardón): perhaps a Galician topographic name based on Castilianized form of xardón ‘holly’ (compare Jardon ). Alternatively, a habitational name from the placename La Alameda de Gardón in Salamanca.

nameMeanings.name: Anders
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Vernacular form of   Andreas .

historicEvents.description: Copenhagen is declared the capital city.

en.lifeSummary: When Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde was born about 1409, in Kettrup, Ålborghus, Denmark, his father, Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard, was 42 and his mother, Gertsdatter Ulfstand, was 33. He married Alice Lovell about 1433, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 13 October 1454, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46, and was buried in Wymondham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde est né environ 1409, à Kettrup, Ålborghus, Danemark, son père, Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Gertsdatter Ulfstand, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Alice Lovell environ 1433, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 13 octobre 1454, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Wymondham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde um 1409 in Kettrup, Ålborghus, Dänemark geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard 42 und seine Mutter Gertsdatter Ulfstand 33. Er hat um 1433 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Alice Lovell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 13. Oktober 1454 im Alter von 46 Jahren in England, United Kingdom und wurde in Wymondham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde è nato il circa 1409 nel Kettrup, Ålborghus, Danimarca, il suo padre, Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Gertsdatter Ulfstand, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Alice Lovell circa 1433, nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 13 ottobre 1454, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Wymondham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogardeが約1409年にデンマークaルボルグハスケットルップで生まれた時，父Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaardは42歳，母Gertsdatter Ulfstandは33歳であった。約1433年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでAlice Lovellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1454年10月13日にイギリスイギリスで46歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーフォルクウイモンドハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde(이)가 대략 1409년 덴마크 a볼그후스 케트루프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Gertsdatter Ulfstand(은)는 33세였다. 그는 대략 1433년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 Alice Lovell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1454년 10월 13일 영국 잉글랜드에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 윈이몽함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde nasceu aproximadamente 1409, em Kettrup, Ålborghus, Dinamarca, seu pai, Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Gertsdatter Ulfstand, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Lovell aproximadamente 1433, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde родился около 1409 г. в местности кеттруп, aлборгус, Дания, его отцу по имени Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Gertsdatter Ulfstand, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Lovell около 1433 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 13 октября 1454 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности вимондхам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Anders Pedersen Gyldenstierne a.k.a. Sir Andrew Ogarde nació aproximadamente 1409, en Kettrup, Ålborghus, Dinamarca, su papá, Sir Peder Nielsen Gyldenstierne Af Aagaard, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Gertsdatter Ulfstand, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Alice Lovell aproximadamente 1433, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 13 de octubre de 1454, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 46 años, y fue sepultado en Wymondham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
